rationale.R1.2: The section contains logical gaps: the decoder configuration is described as 'appearing to perform reasonably well' without grounding, and the ablation conclusion ('seems to suggest') is weakly connected to its premise — consistency between experimental claims and stated conclusions needs tightening.
rationale.R1.3: Load-bearing: the section reports concrete benchmark numbers (WER 3.1% / 7.4%, 0.6 WER ablation delta) but hedges comparison to prior work so heavily that the evidence-claim link is broken. The numbers need to be anchored to specific cited baselines with actual deltas.
rationale.R1.4: The delta vs. prior work is the crux of the results paragraph yet is entirely unquantified ('roughly comparable to or perhaps slightly better than several recently reported numbers'). Identifying the nearest comparators and stating precise gaps is central to making the novelty claim honest and verifiable.
rationale.R1.6: Pervasive hedging chains ('possibly promising direction that, in our view, may merit', 'tentatively interpret as a reasonable result', 'seemed to work fairly well') degrade both readability and credibility. Sentence-level revision is needed throughout to replace vague qualifiers with precise statements or explicit confidence bounds.
